entries.name: invertMaybeBoolean
entries.description: If a non-null boolean value is provided, returns the opposite.

params.name: x
params.description: - the boolean value to invert, or a nullish value

entries.returns: the inverted boolean, or the original nullish value if input was null/undefined

entries.name: reduceBooleansWithAnd
entries.description: Reduces an array of booleans with the logical AND operation.

params.name: array
params.description: - Array of boolean values to reduce.

params.name: emptyArrayValue
params.description: - Value to return if the array is empty. If not provided and the array is empty, a TypeError will be thrown.

entries.returns: The result of ANDing all boolean values in the array.
entries.example:
reduceBooleansWithAnd([true, true, true]);   // true
reduceBooleansWithAnd([true, false, true]);   // false
reduceBooleansWithAnd([], true);              // true (emptyArrayValue)

entries.name: reduceBooleansWithOr
entries.description: Reduces an array of booleans with the logical OR operation.

params.name: array
params.description: - Array of boolean values to reduce.

params.name: emptyArrayValue
params.description: - Value to return if the array is empty. If not provided and the array is empty, a TypeError will be thrown.

entries.returns: The result of ORing all boolean values in the array.

entries.name: booleanFactory
entries.description: Creates a new BooleanFactory that generates random boolean values based on chance.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for the boolean factory, including the chance of returning true.

entries.returns: A factory function (`BooleanFactory`) that generates random boolean values based on the configured chance.

entries.name: randomBoolean
entries.description: Returns a random boolean based on the specified chance.

params.name: chance
params.description: - Number between 0.0 and 100.0 representing the percentage chance of returning true (default: 50, i.e., 50%).

entries.returns: A random boolean value with the specified probability of being true.

entries.name: stringToBoolean
entries.signature: stringToBoolean(value: Maybe<string | boolean>, defaultValue?: Maybe<boolean>): Maybe<boolean>
entries.description:
Converts the input string to a boolean.

Case-insensitive matches for yes/y/t/true and no/n/f/false.

params.name: value
params.description: - The string to convert to a boolean.

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - The default value to return if the string cannot be converted to a boolean (default: undefined).

entries.returns: The boolean value corresponding to the string, or the default value if the string cannot be converted.
entries.example:
stringToBoolean('yes');          // true
stringToBoolean('FALSE');        // false
stringToBoolean('maybe');        // undefined
stringToBoolean('maybe', true);  // true (defaultValue)

entries.name: trueOrFalseString
entries.description:
Converts a boolean to its `'true'` or `'false'` string representation.
Returns null/undefined if the input is nullish.

params.name: value
params.description: - The boolean value to convert.

entries.returns: The string `'true'` or `'false'`, or the nullish input unchanged.

entries.name: batch
entries.description: Splits the input array into batches of a maximum size. Each batch carries its zero-based index as `.i`.

params.name: input
params.description: - Source items to partition into consecutive runs.

params.name: batchSize
params.description: - Upper bound on items per batch.

entries.returns: Consecutive batches tagged with their zero-based index.

entries.name: batchCalc
entries.description: Calculates batch metrics (count, full batches, remainder) from a {@link BatchCount} configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - The total items and items-per-batch configuration.

entries.returns: A {@link BatchCalc } with computed batch counts and remainder.

entries.name: restoreOrder
entries.signature: restoreOrder(orderKeys: K[], values: T[], { readKey, chooseRetainedValue = (values: T[]) => values[0], excludeNewItems = false }: RestoreOrderParams<T, K>): T[]
entries.description:
Reorders values to match a reference key ordering. Values not present in the order keys
are appended at the end (unless `excludeNewItems` is true). Duplicates are resolved via
the `chooseRetainedValue` function (defaults to keeping the first).

params.name: orderKeys
params.description: - Keys defining the desired order.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to reorder.

params.name: { readKey, chooseRetainedValue = (values: T[]) => values[0], excludeNewItems = false }

entries.returns: The reordered values array.

entries.name: makeKeyPairs
entries.signature: makeKeyPairs(values: T[], keyFn: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>): [Maybe<K>, T][]
entries.description: Creates an array of `[key, value]` tuples by extracting a key from each value.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to create key pairs from.

params.name: keyFn
params.description: - Extracts the key from each value.

entries.returns: Tuples pairing each derived key with its source value.

entries.name: separateValues
entries.description: Separates values into included and excluded groups based on a decision function.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to separate.

params.name: checkInclusion
params.description: - Returns `true` for values that should be included.

entries.returns: A {@link SeparateResult } with included and excluded arrays.

entries.name: groupValues
entries.signature: groupValues(values: Maybe<T[]>, groupKeyFn: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>): GroupingResult<T>
entries.description: Groups values by key into a plain object. Convenience wrapper around {@link makeValuesGroupMap} that returns a POJO instead of a Map.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to group.

params.name: groupKeyFn
params.description: - Extracts the grouping key from each value.

entries.returns: A plain object mapping each key to its array of values.

entries.name: makeValuesGroupMap
entries.signature: makeValuesGroupMap(values: Maybe<T[]>, groupKeyFn: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>): Map<Maybe<K>, T[]>
entries.description: Groups values by key into a Map. Each key maps to the array of values that produced that key.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to group.

params.name: groupKeyFn
params.description: - Extracts the grouping key from each value.

entries.returns: A Map from each key to its array of values.

entries.name: decodeHashedValues
entries.signature: decodeHashedValues(hashedValues: string[], decodeValues: string[], hashFn: (value: string) => string): string[]
entries.description: Decodes a list of hashed string values using a provided list of potential original values and a hash function.

params.name: hashedValues
params.description: - Hashed inputs awaiting reverse lookup.

params.name: decodeValues
params.description: - Plaintext candidates that may match one of the hashes.

params.name: hashFn
params.description: - Deterministic hashing used to align the candidate set with the input hashes.

entries.returns: Plaintext recovered from `decodeValues`, dropping any hash that lacks a match.

entries.name: makeHashDecodeMap
entries.signature: makeHashDecodeMap(decodeValues: string[], hashFn: (value: string) => string): HashDecodeMap
entries.description:
Creates a `HashDecodeMap` from a list of potential original string values and a hash function.
The map's keys are the hashed versions of the `decodeValues`, and the values are the original `decodeValues`.

params.name: decodeValues
params.description: - Plaintext candidates whose hashes the map should index.

params.name: hashFn
params.description: - Deterministic hashing used to compute each key.

entries.returns: Hash-to-plaintext lookup ready for reverse decoding.

entries.name: hashStringToNumber
entries.description:
Computes a stable, non-negative 32-bit integer hash for the input string using the FNV-1a algorithm.

Deterministic and dependency-free (no `Math.random`): the same input always yields the same value,
making it suitable for deterministically mapping a string onto a fixed-size set (e.g. picking a
curated color for a name via `hashStringToNumber(value) % colors.length`).

params.name: value
params.description: - String to hash.

entries.returns: A non-negative integer in the range `[0, 2^32)`.
entries.example:
hashStringToNumber('Michelle B'); // stable integer, same every call
hashStringToNumber('Michelle B') % 12; // deterministic bucket index 0-11

entries.name: iterate
entries.description: Iterates over each value in the array sequentially, awaiting each callback before proceeding to the next.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of values to iterate over.

params.name: useFn
params.description: - Callback invoked for each value.

entries.returns: Promise<void>

entries.name: readKeysFunction
entries.signature: readKeysFunction(readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K> | ReadMultipleKeysFunction<T, K>): ReadKeysFunction<T, K>
entries.description:
Creates a {@link ReadKeysFunction} from a key-reading function. Handles both single and array inputs,
filtering out null/undefined keys.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function that extracts one or more keys from a value.

entries.returns: Reusable extractor that normalizes single or batch input into a flat key list (nullish keys dropped).
entries.example:
const fn = readKeysFunction<string>((x) => x);
fn(['a', 'b', 'c']); // ['a', 'b', 'c']

entries.name: readKeysSetFunction
entries.signature: readKeysSetFunction(readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K> | ReadMultipleKeysFunction<T, K>): ReadKeysSetFunction<T, K>
entries.description: Creates a {@link ReadKeysSetFunction} from a key-reading function. Like {@link readKeysFunction} but returns a Set, deduplicating keys.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function that extracts one or more keys from a value.

entries.returns: Reusable extractor that collects keys into a deduped Set.
entries.example:
const fn = readKeysSetFunction<string>((x) => x);
fn(['a', 'b', 'a']); // Set { 'a', 'b' }

entries.name: reverseCompareFn
entries.description: Convenience function that reverses the order of the sorted values.

params.name: compareFn
params.description: - the comparison function whose order should be reversed

entries.returns: a new comparison function with the opposite sort direction

entries.name: compareFnOrder
entries.description:
Convenience function that reverses the order of the sorted values if the order is specified descending.

The input comparison function must be in ascending order.

params.name: ascendingCompareFn
params.description: - A comparison function that sorts in ascending order.

params.name: order
params.description: - The desired sort direction; defaults to 'asc'.

entries.returns: The original function if ascending, or a reversed version if descending.

entries.name: compareWithMappedValuesFunction
entries.signature: compareWithMappedValuesFunction(mapValue: MapFunction<T, V>, comparesFunction: SortCompareFunction<V>): SortCompareFunction<T>
entries.description:
Creates a {@link SortCompareFunction} that maps values to a different type before comparing.
Useful for sorting objects by a derived property.

params.name: mapValue
params.description: - Maps each value to the type used for comparison.

params.name: comparesFunction
params.description: - Compares the mapped values.

entries.returns: A sort comparison function for the original type.

entries.name: minAndMaxFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link MinAndMaxFunction} that finds the minimum and maximum values from an iterable using the provided comparison function.

params.name: compareFn
params.description: - Ascending sort comparison function used to determine min/max.

entries.returns: Returns `{ min, max }` or `null` for empty iterables.

entries.name: isObjectWithConstructor
entries.description:
Returns true if the input is a function-like value with a prototype and constructor (i.e., a class or named function declaration).
Returns false for arrow functions, class instances, plain objects, and primitives.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a function with a constructor.

entries.name: isClassLikeType
entries.description:
Returns true if the input is a class (requires `new` to instantiate). Distinguishes classes from regular functions
by checking that the prototype is non-writable.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a class type.

entries.name: getFunctionType
entries.description:
Determines the function type of the input value: `'class'`, `'function'`, or `'arrow'`.
Returns `null` if the input is not a function.

params.name: x
params.description: - The value to inspect.

entries.returns: The {@link FunctionType }, or `null` for non-functions.

entries.name: isNonClassFunction
entries.description: Returns true if the input is a function but not a class (i.e., a regular function or arrow function).

params.name: x
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a non-class function.

entries.name: arrayFactory
entries.description: Creates a new ArrayFactory that generates multiple values.

params.name: factory
params.description: - Per-item producer invoked once per requested element.

entries.returns: Count-driven generator that materializes the requested number of items.

entries.name: arrayInputFactory
entries.description: Creates an ArrayInputFactory that transforms input values into output values.

params.name: factory
params.description: - Per-element producer invoked with each input value plus its index.

entries.returns: Adapter that runs `factory` against every input element to produce the mapped output array.

entries.name: makeBestFit
entries.description: Same as applyBestFit, but returns a new array, rather than modifying the existing array.

params.name: input
params.description: - Source items considered for best-fit selection; not mutated.

params.name: config
params.description: - Strategy describing eligibility, comparison, and non-winner transformation.

entries.returns: Fresh array containing the chosen winner alongside transformed runners-up.

entries.name: applyBestFit
entries.description:
Used for updating an array so that a single element becomes the "best fit" in whatever context is provided.

For instance, if two items are selected but only one can be selected by design, this function can be used to
pick the best fit, and update the input array.

params.name: input
params.description: - Mutable array whose runners-up should be rewritten in place.

params.name: config
params.description: - Strategy describing eligibility, comparison, and non-winner transformation.

entries.returns: Same `input` reference after the runner-up replacements.

entries.name: filterAndMapFunction
entries.signature: filterAndMapFunction(decisionFunction: DecisionFunction<I>, mapFunction: MapFunction<I, O>): FilterAndMapFunction<I, O>
entries.description:
Creates a function that filters the input values and maps all matching values to a new value.
This is a higher-order function that combines filtering and mapping operations.

params.name: decisionFunction
params.description: - Predicate used to decide whether each iterated item flows through.

params.name: mapFunction
params.description: - Transform applied to every included item.

entries.returns: Reusable filter-then-map operator over any iterable input.

entries.name: arrayDecisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link ArrayDecisionFunction} from a per-element predicate and a {@link SetIncludesMode}.

When mode is `'any'`, the resulting function returns `true` if at least one element satisfies the predicate.
When mode is `'all'`, it returns `true` only if every element satisfies the predicate.

params.name: decision
params.description: - Predicate used to test individual elements.

params.name: mode
params.description: - Whether all or any elements must satisfy the predicate.

entries.returns: Predicate operating on whole arrays driven by the configured aggregation mode.

entries.name: arrayDecision
entries.signature: arrayDecision(values: T[], decision: ArrayFindDecisionFunction<T>, mode: SetIncludesMode): boolean
entries.description: Convenience wrapper that creates and immediately invokes an {@link ArrayDecisionFunction}.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array to evaluate.

params.name: decision
params.description: - Predicate used to test individual elements.

params.name: mode
params.description: - Whether all or any elements must satisfy the predicate.

entries.returns: `true` if the array satisfies the decision criteria for the given mode.

entries.name: sliceIndexRangeFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link SliceIndexRangeFunction} that slices the specified index range from any input array.

params.name: inputRange
params.description: - Range boundaries to bake into the returned slicer.

entries.returns: Reusable slicer that extracts the configured range from any input array.

entries.name: rangedIndexedValuesArrayAccessorFactory
entries.description:
Creates a factory that produces {@link RangedIndexedValuesArrayAccessor} instances.

Each accessor maps an index to the value whose range contains that index, or undefined if no range matches.

params.name: readIndexRange
params.description: - Function that reads the index range from each value.

entries.returns: A factory that creates ranged accessors from arrays of values.

entries.name: indexedValuesArrayAccessorFactory
entries.description:
Creates a factory that produces {@link IndexedValuesArrayAccessor} instances.

Each accessor maps an index to the matching value, falling back to the previous value, then the next value.
This guarantees a value is always returned.

params.name: readIndexRange
params.description: - Function that reads the index range from each value.

entries.returns: A factory that creates indexed accessors from arrays of values.

entries.name: rangedIndexedValuesArrayAccessorInfoFactory
entries.description:
Creates a factory that produces {@link RangedIndexedValuesArrayInfoAccessor} instances.

Each accessor sorts the values by their index ranges in ascending order, then for a given index
returns the matching value along with its previous and next neighbors.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration containing the index range reader function.

entries.returns: A factory that creates ranged info accessors from arrays of values.

entries.name: limitArray
entries.description:
Limits the number of items in an array based on the provided configuration.
Items are taken from the front of the array by default, or from the end if configured.

params.name: array
params.description: - source array to limit

params.name: inputConfig
params.description: - configuration controlling the limit count and direction

entries.returns: a new array with at most the configured number of items, or the original array if no limit is specified

entries.name: randomArrayFactory
entries.description: Creates a factory function that generates arrays of a random length populated with items from a make function.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration containing the make function and random number source.

entries.returns: A factory that produces arrays of random length, optionally accepting a specific count override.

entries.name: randomPickFactory
entries.description: Creates a {@link RandomPickFactory} from the input values.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of values to randomly pick from.

entries.returns: A callable factory that returns a random value from the array on each invocation.

entries.name: randomArrayIndex
entries.description: Returns a random index from the input array. Returns 0 if the array is empty.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array to generate a random index for.

entries.returns: A random valid index within the array, or 0 if the array is empty.

entries.name: pickOneRandomly
entries.description: Picks a single item randomly from the input array.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array to pick a random item from.

entries.returns: A randomly selected item from the array.

entries.name: keepValuesFromArray
entries.description: Returns items that exist in both arrays (intersection).

params.name: values
params.description: - Source whose entries are kept only when present in the second array.

params.name: secondArray
params.description: - Allowed-values gate used for the intersection check.

entries.returns: Intersection of `values` and `secondArray`, ordered like `values`.

entries.name: excludeValuesFromArray
entries.description: Returns items from the first array that do not exist in the second array (difference).

params.name: values
params.description: - Source whose entries are kept only when absent from the second array.

params.name: secondArray
params.description: - Excluded-values gate used for the difference check.

entries.returns: Set difference `values \ secondArray`, ordered like `values`.

entries.name: arrayContainsDuplicateValue
entries.description: Checks whether the given array contains any duplicate values.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source to scan for any repeated entry.

entries.returns: True when at least one value appears more than once; otherwise false.

entries.name: findIndexOfFirstDuplicateValue
entries.description: Finds the index of the first duplicate value in the given array.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source to scan left-to-right for the first repeat.

entries.returns: Index of the first value that matches an earlier one, or `-1` when all values are unique.

entries.name: convertMaybeToNonEmptyArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is a non-empty array. Returns undefined if the input is nullish or results in an empty array.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - Single value or array to convert.

entries.returns: An array with at least one element, or undefined if the result would be empty.

entries.name: convertMaybeToArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is an array. Returns an empty array if the input is nullish.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - Single value, array, or nullish value to convert.

entries.returns: The input wrapped in an array, the input array itself, or an empty array if nullish.

entries.name: convertToArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is already an array.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - Single value or array to convert.

entries.returns: The input array unchanged, or a new single-element array wrapping the input value.

entries.name: firstValue
entries.description: Returns the first value from the array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - Single value or array to retrieve from.

entries.returns: The first element of the array, or the input value itself.

entries.name: lastValue
entries.description: Returns the last value from the array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - Single value or array to retrieve from.

entries.returns: The last element of the array, or the input value itself.

entries.name: firstAndLastValue
entries.description:
Returns a tuple with the first and last value of the input.

If the input is not an array, returns that value as both the first and last value.

params.name: input
params.description: - Single value or array to retrieve from.

entries.returns: A two-element tuple of the first and last values.

entries.name: valueAtIndex
entries.description: Returns the value at the given index from an array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - Single value or array to retrieve from.

params.name: index
params.description: - Zero-based index of the element to retrieve.

entries.returns: The element at the specified index, or the input value itself if not an array.

entries.name: concatArrays
entries.description: Concatenates the input arrays into a single array, filtering out nullish entries.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - Arrays to concatenate; nullish entries are ignored.

entries.returns: A single flattened array containing all elements from the non-nullish input arrays.

entries.name: flattenArray
entries.description: Flattens a two-dimensional array into a single-dimensional array. Any null/undefined entries in the outer dimension are filtered out.

params.name: array
params.description: - Two-dimensional array to flatten, may contain nullish entries.

entries.returns: A single-dimensional array with all elements from the non-nullish inner arrays.

entries.name: copyArray
entries.description: Creates a shallow copy of the input array. Returns an empty array if the input is nullish.

params.name: input
params.description: - Array to copy, or nullish.

entries.returns: A new array with the same elements, or an empty array if input is nullish.

entries.name: mergeArrays
entries.description: Merges all input arrays into a single new array. Nullish entries are ignored.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - Arrays to merge; nullish entries are skipped.

entries.returns: A new array containing all elements from the provided arrays.

entries.name: takeFront
entries.description: Copies/takes the elements from the front of the array up to the specified maximum.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source array to take from.

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: - Maximum number of elements to take from the front.

entries.returns: A new array containing at most maxToTake elements from the front.

entries.name: splitFront
entries.description: Splits the array into two arrays, the first being the front of the array up to the maxToTake, and the second being the remaining values.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source items to partition into front + remaining halves.

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: - Upper bound on how many entries to take from the start.

entries.returns: Pair containing the leading slice plus everything left over.

entries.name: takeLast
entries.signature: takeLast(values: T[], maxToTake: number, keepFromFront?: number): T[]
entries.description: Copies/takes as many elements as possible from the end.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to take from.

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: - Max number of values to take from the end of the input array.

params.name: keepFromFront
params.description: - Number of values to retain in the front of the array. These are not taken.

entries.returns: New array with the subset of taken values.

entries.name: countAllInNestedArray
entries.description: Counts the total number of elements across all inner arrays of a nested array.

params.name: array
params.description: - Two-dimensional array whose elements are counted.

entries.returns: The total number of elements across all inner arrays.

entries.name: removeValuesAtIndexesFromArrayCopy
entries.description: Creates a copy of the array with the items at the specified indexes removed.

params.name: array
params.description: - Source array to copy from.

params.name: removeIndexes
params.description: - Indexes of elements to exclude from the copy.

entries.returns: A new array without the elements at the specified indexes.

entries.name: concatArraysUnique
entries.description: Concatenates multiple arrays and returns only unique values.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - Arrays to concatenate. Null/undefined arrays are ignored.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values from all input arrays.

entries.name: flattenArrayUnique
entries.description: Flattens a 2D array and returns only unique values.

params.name: array
params.description: - Two-dimensional array to flatten and deduplicate.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values from the flattened input.

entries.name: unique
entries.signature: unique(values: T[], excludeInput?: FilterUniqueFunctionExcludeKeysInput<T, T>): T[]
entries.description: Filters the input values and only returns unique values.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of primitive-key values to deduplicate.

params.name: excludeInput
params.description: - Optional keys or values to exclude from the result.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values with exclusions removed.

entries.name: filterUniqueFunction
entries.signature: filterUniqueFunction(readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>, additionalKeysInput?: FilterUniqueFunctionAdditionalKeysInput<T, K>): FilterUniqueFunction<T, K>
entries.description: Creates a {@link FilterUniqueFunction} that deduplicates items by their computed key.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

params.name: additionalKeysInput
params.description: - Optional keys or values to pre-seed as already seen, causing them to be excluded.

entries.returns: A reusable filter function that removes duplicate items from arrays.

entries.name: filterUniqueValues
entries.signature: filterUniqueValues(values: T[], readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>, additionalKeys?: K[]): T[]
entries.description: Filters an array to contain only items with unique keys.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of items to deduplicate.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

params.name: additionalKeys
params.description: - Optional keys to pre-seed as already seen, excluding matching items.

entries.returns: Array containing only the first occurrence of each uniquely-keyed item.

entries.name: isUniqueKeyedFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IsUniqueKeyedFunction} that checks whether all items in an array have unique keys.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

entries.returns: A decision function that returns true if all items have distinct keys.

entries.name: allowValueOnceFilter
entries.description: Creates a new {@link AllowValueOnceFilter} that permits each unique key only on its first encounter.

params.name: inputReadKey
params.description: - Optional function to extract a key from each value. Defaults to identity.

entries.returns: A stateful filter function that returns true only for the first occurrence of each key.

entries.name: filterMaybeArrayFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link FilterMaybeArrayFunction} that filters maybe values from an array using the provided filter function.

params.name: filterFn
params.description: - Filter predicate used to determine which values to keep.

entries.returns: Reusable filter operator that drops nullish entries and applies the predicate to the rest.

entries.name: filterMaybeArrayValues
entries.description: Filters all maybe values from the input array. If a maybe value is input, returns an empty array.
entries.returns: An array containing only non-null and non-undefined values.

entries.name: filterEmptyArrayValues
entries.description: Filters all empty and maybe values from the input array. If a maybe value is input, returns an empty array.
entries.returns: An array containing only non-null, non-undefined, and non-empty values.

entries.name: authRoleClaimsService
entries.description:
Creates an {@link AuthRoleClaimsService} that converts between {@link AuthRoleSet} and JWT-style claims objects.

Each key in the config maps a claim key to role(s). Simple entries map a claim value to one or more roles,
while encode/decode entries allow custom bidirectional conversion logic.

params.name: config
params.description: - Mapping of claim keys to their role configuration entries (or null to ignore)

params.name: defaults
params.description: - Optional default values for claim presence and absence.

entries.name: memoizeAsyncValueCache
entries.description:
Wraps an inner {@link AsyncValueCache} with a single-load in-memory memoization layer.

The first {@link AsyncValueCache.load} call delegates to the inner cache and stores the
result; subsequent calls return the memoized value without touching the inner cache again.
{@link AsyncValueCache.update} writes through to the inner cache and refreshes the memo.
{@link AsyncValueCache.clear} clears both layers.

Note: the memoized value is per-process. Long-running processes will not observe writes
made by other processes to the inner backing once the memo is populated.

params.name: inner
params.description: - The backing cache to memoize. Reads are delegated once and cached; writes are forwarded through and refresh the memo.

entries.returns: An {@link AsyncValueCache } that proxies the inner cache with a single-load memoization layer.
entries.example:
const memo = memoizeAsyncValueCache(inMemoryAsyncValueCache<string>('initial'));
await memo.load();           // delegates to inner.load() once
await memo.load();           // returns memoized value without hitting inner
await memo.update('next');   // writes through to inner and refreshes memo

entries.name: memoizeAsyncKeyedValueCache
entries.description:
Wraps an inner {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache} with a single-load in-memory memoization layer
over the full record.

The first call to any read method ({@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.load} or {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.get})
delegates to the inner cache and stores the loaded record; subsequent reads return entries
from the memoized record without re-hitting the inner cache. Writes ({@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.set} /
{@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.remove}) update the memoized record and write through to the inner cache.
{@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.clear} clears both layers.

Note: the memoized record is per-process. Long-running processes will not observe writes
made by other processes to the inner backing once the memo is populated.

params.name: inner
params.description: - The backing keyed cache to memoize. The full record is loaded once and cached; writes are forwarded through and applied to the memo.

entries.returns: An {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache } that proxies the inner cache with a record-level memoization layer.
entries.example:
const memo = memoizeAsyncKeyedValueCache(inMemoryAsyncKeyedValueCache<number>({ a: 1 }));
await memo.get('a');           // delegates to inner.load() once
await memo.get('a');           // returns memoized entry without hitting inner
await memo.set('b', 2);        // writes through to inner and updates memo

entries.name: expirationDetails
entries.description:
Returns an {@link ExpirationDetails} for the given input configuration.

Use this when you need to ask whether something has expired or what its expiration date is. See {@link ExpirationDetailsInput} for how the expiration date is resolved (`expires` → `expiresAt` → `expiresFromDate + expiresIn`).

Common patterns:
- **Direct expiration**: `expirationDetails({ expiresAt }).hasExpired()`
- **Wrap an existing object**: `expirationDetails({ expires: token }).hasExpired()`
- **TTL / throttle**: `expirationDetails({ expiresFromDate: lastRunAt, expiresIn: throttleMs })` — see {@link isThrottled}
- **Pre-emptive refresh**: `expirationDetails({ expiresFromDate: expiresAt, expiresIn: -bufferMs })` — treat as expired `bufferMs` before the real expiration, e.g. to refresh a token before it actually dies

params.name: input
params.description: - Configuration for calculating expiration.

entries.returns: An ExpirationDetails object that can determine expiration state.

entries.name: calculateExpirationDate
entries.description:
Convenience function for calculating and returning the expiration date given the input.
This is a shorthand for expirationDetails(input).getExpirationDate().

params.name: input
params.description: - Input configuration used to calculate the expiration date.

entries.returns: The calculated expiration date, or null if no expiration is defined.

entries.name: isExpired
entries.description: Convenience wrapper around {@link expirationDetails}().hasExpired() that treats null/undefined input or no expiration date as expired.

params.name: input
params.description: - Expiration configuration. Null/undefined is treated as expired.

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional override for the current time. Defaults to the current time. Apply any buffer (e.g. for clock skew or pre-emptive refresh) by shifting this value forward.

entries.returns: True when the input is null/undefined or its expiration date has passed; otherwise false.
entries.example:
isExpired(null); // true
isExpired({ expiresAt: pastDate }); // true
isExpired({ expiresAt: futureDate }); // false
isExpired({ expiresAt: futureDate }, addMilliseconds(new Date(), 60_000)); // true (when within buffer)

entries.name: isUnderThreshold
entries.signature: isUnderThreshold(threshold: Milliseconds, nextRunAt: Maybe<DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber>, now?: Maybe<Date>): boolean
entries.description:
Returns true if the threshold has not passed since the next run time, compared to now.

Used in cases where a date that was calculated using the "threshold" value is given and we want to check if we are still within that threshold.

Example:
- Should send a notification at max every 2 days. The threshold is 2 days in milliseconds, and "nextRunAt" is the previously calculated date that was originally "now" + "threshold".

params.name: threshold
params.description: - The threshold time. Typically this is amount of time that was used to calculate the original "nextRunAt" time.

params.name: nextRunAt
params.description: - Time the next run will occur. If null/undefined, then this function will return false.

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional override for the current time. Defaults to the current time.

entries.returns: True if the threshold has not passed since the next run time, compared to now.

entries.name: isThrottled
entries.signature: isThrottled(throttleTime: Maybe<Milliseconds>, lastRunAt: Maybe<DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber>, now?: Maybe<Date>): boolean
entries.description:
Convenience function for quickly calculating throttling given a throttle time and last run time.

Returns true if the throttle time has not passed since the last run time, compared to now.
This is useful for rate limiting operations (e.g., "only allow this action once every X milliseconds").

params.name: throttleTime
params.description: - Minimum time in milliseconds that must pass between operations.

params.name: lastRunAt
params.description: - Timestamp when the operation was last performed.

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional override for the current time (defaults to the current time)

entries.returns: True if the operation should be throttled (not enough time has passed), false otherwise.

entries.description: Creates a KeyValueMapFactory that maps values by their key using a ReadKeyFunction.

params.name: read
params.description: - Function that extracts a key from each value.

entries.returns: A factory that creates Maps from arrays of values.

entries.name: multiKeyValueMapFactory
entries.description:
Creates a KeyValueMapFactory that maps values by multiple keys using a ReadMultipleKeysFunction.
Each value can appear under multiple keys.

params.name: read
params.description: - Function that extracts multiple keys from each value.

entries.returns: A factory that creates Maps from arrays of values.

entries.name: idBatchFactory
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IdBatchFactory} that generates batches of unique, verified identifiers.

The factory generates identifiers in batches, filters them for uniqueness, and verifies each batch
using the configured verifier. Throws if uniqueness generation fails repeatedly (after 20 attempts).

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with the base factory for generating candidates and the verifier for validating them.

entries.returns: An async factory function that produces the requested number of valid identifiers.

entries.name: sequentialIncrementingNumberStringModelIdFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link ModelIdFactory} that generates sequential incrementing encoded string identifiers.

Each call to the returned factory produces the next identifier in the sequence, encoded using the configured
{@link NumberStringDencoder} (defaults to base-64). Supports starting from a specific index or continuing
from a current index, and an optional transform for post-processing (e.g., padding).

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for the starting index, increment step, dencoder, and transform.

entries.returns: A factory function that returns the next encoded string identifier on each call.

entries.name: makeModelMapFunctions
entries.description:
Creates bidirectional map functions (`from` and `to`) from a set of {@link ModelFieldConversions}.

The `to` function converts from the model (V) to data (D), while `from` converts back from data (D) to model (V).

params.name: fields
params.description: - Field conversion definitions for each key in the model.

entries.returns: Object with `from` and `to` mapping functions.

entries.name: makeModelConversionFieldValuesFunction
entries.description:
Creates a conversion function that maps fields from an input object to an output object using per-field conversion functions.

Supports optional filtering by field names and skipping undefined values via {@link ModelConversionOptions}.

params.name: fields
params.description: - Array of `[key, convertFn]` tuples defining how each field is converted.

entries.returns: Reusable converter that applies the per-field transforms to any input record.

entries.name: modelFieldMapFunctions
entries.description: Compiles a {@link ModelFieldMapFunctionsConfig} into resolved {@link ModelFieldMapFunctions} with `from` and `to` mapping functions.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with `from` and `to` field map configs.

entries.returns: Compiled field map functions.

entries.name: modelFieldMapFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link ModelFieldMapFunction} from a config that handles Maybe input values.

The function handles three cases:
- Non-null input: uses `convert` to transform the value
- Null/undefined input with `convertMaybe`: delegates to that function
- Null/undefined input with `default` or `defaultInput`: uses the appropriate fallback

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration specifying how to convert values and handle null/undefined.

entries.returns: Reusable mapper that handles concrete and nullish inputs via the configured fallbacks.

entries.name: toModelMapFunctions
entries.description:
Resolves a {@link ToModelMapFunctionsInput} to {@link ModelMapFunctions}.

Accepts a pre-built `mapFunctions` reference, a `fieldConversions` ref, or a `fields` config.

params.name: input
params.description: - Input that can be resolved to model map functions.

entries.returns: Bidirectional model map functions.

entries.name: makeCopyModelFieldFunction
entries.signature: makeCopyModelFieldFunction(key: keyof T, inputConfig?: Maybe<CopyModelFieldConfig>): CopyModelFieldFunction<T>
entries.description:
Creates a function that copies a single field from a partial source object to a partial target object.

If the field exists on the source, its value (or the configured default if null) is assigned to the target.
If the field does not exist on the source but a default is configured, the default is used.
Otherwise, the target is left unchanged.

params.name: key
params.description: - The property key to copy.

params.name: inputConfig
params.description: - Optional config with a default value for the field.

entries.returns: Reusable mapper that copies the configured field (or fills in the default) per pair.

entries.name: modifyModelMapFunctions
entries.description:
Wraps existing {@link ModelMapFunctions} with modifier functions that are applied to the input before conversion.

Optionally copies the input object before modification to avoid mutating the original.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with the base map functions, modifiers, and copy options.

entries.returns: New model map functions with modifiers applied before each conversion.

entries.name: modifyModelMapFunction
entries.signature: modifyModelMapFunction(mapFn: ModelMapFunction<I, O>, modifyModel: Maybe<ModifierFunction<I>>, copy?: boolean): ModelMapFunction<I, O>
entries.description:
Wraps a single {@link ModelMapFunction} with a modifier that is applied to the input before the map function runs.

When `copy` is true (default), the input is shallow-copied before modification to avoid mutating the original.
If no modifier is provided, the original map function is returned unchanged.

params.name: mapFn
params.description: - The base map function to wrap.

params.name: modifyModel
params.description: - Optional modifier to apply before mapping.

params.name: copy
params.description: - Whether to shallow-copy the input before modifying; defaults to true.

entries.returns: The wrapped map function, or the original if no modifier is provided.

entries.name: makeModelMap
entries.signature: makeModelMap(input: T[], read?: ReadModelKeyFunction<T>): Map<Maybe<ModelKey>, T>
entries.description:
Creates a Map from model key to model, indexing each model by its key.

If multiple models share the same key, the last one wins.

params.name: input
params.description: - Array of models to index

params.name: read
params.description: - Function to extract the key from each model; defaults to reading the `id` property

entries.returns: Map from model key to model

entries.name: makeMultiModelKeyMap
entries.description:
Creates a Map that indexes each model by multiple keys, allowing lookup of a model by any of its relation keys.

If multiple models share the same relation key, the last one wins for that key.

params.name: input
params.description: - Array of models to index.

params.name: read
params.description: - Function that returns an array of relation keys for each model.

entries.returns: Map from relation key to model.

entries.name: modelTypeDataPairFactory
entries.signature: modelTypeDataPairFactory(typeReader: ReadModelTypeFunction<T, M>, defaultType?: string): ModelTypeDataPairFactory<T, M>
entries.description:
Creates a factory function that wraps input data into a {@link ModelTypeDataPair} by reading the type from the data.

Falls back to the provided default type if the type reader returns a nullish value.

params.name: typeReader
params.description: - Function to extract the model type from input data.

params.name: defaultType
params.description: - Fallback type string when the reader returns nullish.

entries.returns: Factory function that produces ModelTypeDataPair values.

entries.name: readableStreamToStringFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that reads a Node.js ReadableStream and converts its contents to a string using the specified encoding.

params.name: encoding
params.description: - The buffer encoding to use (e.g., 'utf-8', 'base64')

entries.returns: Reusable consumer that drains a stream and resolves with its decoded contents.

entries.name: isInNumberBoundFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that checks whether a number falls within the specified inclusive bounds.

params.name: bounds
params.description: - The min/max bounds to test against.

entries.returns: Returns `true` if the input number is within bounds.

entries.name: wrapNumberFunction
entries.description:
Creates a function that wraps numbers around within the specified bounds, like modular arithmetic.

When `fencePosts` is true, wraps to the nearest "fence post" value, extending the wrap range by one in each direction.

params.name: wrapNumberFunctionConfig
params.description: - Configuration with min, max, and optional fence post behavior.

entries.returns: Wraps input numbers into the bounded range.

entries.name: boundNumberFunction
entries.description:
Creates a function that constrains numbers within bounds, either by clamping or wrapping.

When `wrap` is true, uses modular wrapping. Otherwise, clamps values to the min/max range.

params.name: boundNumberFunctionConfig
params.description: - Configuration with min, max, and optional wrap behavior.

entries.returns: Bounds input numbers into the configured range.

entries.name: boundNumber
entries.signature: boundNumber(input: number, min: T, max: T): T
entries.description: Clamps the input number between the min and max values (inclusive).

params.name: input
params.description: - Number to clamp.

params.name: min
params.description: - Minimum allowed value.

params.name: max
params.description: - Maximum allowed value.

entries.returns: The clamped value.

entries.name: dollarAmountStringWithUnitFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that formats dollar amounts as strings with a unit prefix (e.g., "$12.50").

params.name: unit
params.description: - The unit prefix to prepend; defaults to "$".

entries.returns: Formats dollar amounts with the configured unit.

entries.name: incrementingNumberFactory
entries.description: Creates a factory that returns sequentially increasing numbers, starting from a configurable value and incrementing by a configurable step.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with optional `startAt` (default 0) and `increaseBy` (default 1)

entries.returns: A factory function that returns the next number in the sequence on each call.
entries.example:
const factory = incrementingNumberFactory({ startAt: 10, increaseBy: 5 });
factory(); // 10
factory(); // 15
factory(); // 20

entries.name: percentNumberToDecimal
entries.description:
Converts a {@link PercentNumber} (e.g., 5 for 5%) to its decimal equivalent (e.g., 0.05).

Returns 0 for null/undefined input.

params.name: input
params.description: - A percent number value (e.g., 5 means 5%)

entries.returns: The decimal equivalent.

entries.name: percentNumberFromDecimal
entries.description:
Converts a {@link PercentDecimal} (e.g., 0.05 for 5%) to its {@link PercentNumber} equivalent (e.g., 5).

Returns 0 for null/undefined input.

params.name: input
params.description: - A decimal percent value (e.g., 0.05 means 5%)

entries.returns: The percent number equivalent.

entries.name: asNumber
entries.description:
Converts a number, string, or null/undefined value to a number.

Strings are parsed via `Number()`. Null/undefined returns 0.

params.name: input
params.description: - Numeric value, numeric string, or null/undefined.

entries.returns: The numeric value, or 0 for null/undefined.

entries.name: isNumberDivisibleBy
entries.description:
Checks whether the input value is evenly divisible by the divisor.

Treats null/undefined as 0.

params.name: value
params.description: - The number to check.

params.name: divisor
params.description: - The divisor to test against.

entries.returns: `true` if the remainder is zero.

entries.name: nearestDivisibleValues
entries.description: Finds the nearest values that are evenly divisible by the divisor, both above (ceil) and below (floor) the input value.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to find divisible neighbors for.

params.name: divisor
params.description: - The divisor to align to.

entries.returns: Object with the input value, divisor, and the nearest ceil/floor divisible values.

entries.name: isEvenNumber
entries.description: Checks whether the input is an even number.

params.name: value
params.description: - Number to test.

entries.returns: `true` if even.

entries.name: isOddNumber
entries.description: Checks whether the input is an odd number.

params.name: value
params.description: - Number to test.

entries.returns: `true` if odd.

entries.name: sumOfIntegersBetween
entries.description:
Computes the sum of all integers between two values, inclusive, using Gauss's formula.

The `from` value is floored and the `to` value is ceiled before computation.

params.name: from
params.description: - The starting value (floored to nearest integer)

params.name: to
params.description: - The ending value (ceiled to nearest integer)

entries.returns: Sum of all integers in the range.

entries.name: minAndMaxNumber
entries.description: Finds the minimum and maximum values from an iterable of numbers.

params.name: values
params.description: - Iterable of numbers to examine.

entries.returns: Object with `min` and `max` values.

entries.name: getBaseLog
entries.description: Computes the logarithm of `y` with base `x`.

params.name: x
params.description: - The base of the logarithm.

params.name: y
params.description: - The value to compute the logarithm of.

entries.returns: The base-x logarithm of y.

entries.name: randomNumberFactory
entries.description:
Creates a factory that generates random numbers within a configured range.

Accepts either a simple max number or a full config object with min, max, and rounding options.

params.name: maxOrArgs
params.description: - Maximum value (exclusive) or full configuration object.

params.name: roundingInput
params.description: - Optional rounding mode override.

entries.returns: A factory function that produces random numbers within the range.

entries.name: randomNumber
entries.description: Generates a single random number using {@link randomNumberFactory}. Convenience function for one-off usage.

params.name: maxOrArgs
params.description: - Maximum value (exclusive) or full configuration object.

params.name: roundingInput
params.description: - Optional rounding mode.

entries.returns: A single random number.

entries.name: roundingFunction
entries.description: Returns a rounding function for the specified rounding type.

params.name: type
params.description: - The rounding strategy: 'floor', 'ceil', 'round', or 'none'.

entries.returns: The corresponding Math function, or an identity function for 'none'.

entries.name: cutValueToPrecision
entries.description:
Truncates a number (or number string) to the specified decimal precision without rounding.

Accepts strings and null/undefined via {@link asNumber}.

params.name: input
params.description: - Number, number string, or null/undefined.

params.name: precision
params.description: - Number of decimal places to retain.

entries.returns: The truncated number value.

entries.name: cutValueToInteger
entries.description: Truncates a value to an integer by cutting to zero decimal precision.

params.name: input
params.description: - Number, number string, or null/undefined.

entries.returns: The truncated integer value.

entries.name: cutValueToPrecisionFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link CutValueToPrecisionFunction} that truncates values to the configured precision.

params.name: precision
params.description: - Number of decimal places to retain.

params.name: roundingType
params.description: - Rounding strategy; defaults to 'cut' (truncation)

entries.returns: Accepts a number or string and returns the truncated number.

entries.name: roundToPrecisionFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that rounds numbers to the specified precision using a configurable rounding strategy.

params.name: precision
params.description: - Number of decimal places.

params.name: roundFn
params.description: - Rounding strategy; defaults to 'round'. Use 'cut' for truncation.

entries.returns: Rounds numbers to the configured precision.

entries.name: roundNumberToStepFunction
entries.description:
Creates a function that rounds numbers to the nearest step multiple using a configurable rounding strategy.

Accepts either a step number (uses 'ceil' rounding) or a full config with step, rounding type, and origin.

params.name: input
params.description: - Step size or full configuration.

entries.returns: Rounds input numbers to the nearest step.

entries.name: sortByNumberFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link SortCompareFunction} that sorts values in ascending order by a numeric property.

params.name: readNumberFn
params.description: - Function that extracts the numeric value from each item.

entries.returns: A sort comparator function for ascending numeric order.

entries.name: transformNumberFunction
entries.description:
Creates a composite number transform function from a {@link TransformNumberFunctionConfig}.

Chains the configured operations in order: custom transform, step rounding, precision cut, then bounds clamping.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with optional transform, roundToStep, precision, and bounds.

entries.returns: A single function that applies all configured transformations in sequence.

entries.name: objectDeltaArrayCompressor
entries.description:
Creates an {@link ObjectDeltaArrayCompressor} that can compress and expand arrays of objects using delta encoding.

The first object is stored fully. Subsequent objects store only fields that changed from the previous entry.
Null in a delta entry means the field was cleared. Undefined (missing key) means no change.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with the equality checker that defines which fields to track.

entries.returns: A compressor with `compress` and `expand` methods.

entries.name: objectIsEmpty
entries.description:
Recursively checks whether an object is "empty" — meaning it is null/undefined, has no keys,
or all of its values are themselves empty (recursively for nested objects, or falsy for primitives).

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the object is considered empty.

entries.name: areEqualPOJOValues
entries.description:
Performs a deep equality comparison between two values.

Recursively compares arrays, objects, Maps, Sets, primitives, and Dates.

params.name: a
params.description: - First value to compare.

params.name: b
params.description: - Second value to compare.

entries.returns: `true` if the values are deeply equal.

entries.name: objectFieldEqualityChecker
entries.description:
Creates an {@link ObjectFieldEqualityChecker} that compares two objects field-by-field using configured equality functions.

Fields can be specified as simple field names (using the default `===` comparator) or as config objects with custom comparators.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with the fields to compare and an optional default equality function.

entries.returns: Compares two objects and reports which fields are equal/unequal.

entries.name: stripObjectFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link StripObjectFunction} that filters values from an object and returns
`undefined` when no keys remain after filtering.

Useful when an upstream consumer treats an "empty" object as a missing value (e.g. for skipping
persistence or omitting a field from a request body).

params.name: filter
params.description: - Filter controlling which key/value pairs are removed from the input object.

params.name: copy
params.description: - When true (default), the returned function shallow-copies the input before filtering instead of mutating it.

entries.returns: Returns the stripped object, or `undefined` when filtering removed every key.

entries.name: overrideInObjectFunctionFactory
entries.signature: overrideInObjectFunctionFactory({ filter, copy, dynamic = false }: OverrideInObjectFunctionFactoryConfig<T>): OverrideInObjectFunctionFactory<T>
entries.description:
Creates an {@link OverrideInObjectFunctionFactory} that merges source objects into a template,
then applies that template to target objects.

The template is built by iterating the source objects in order (later sources override earlier ones),
filtering each through the configured filter. When applied to a target, the template's values overwrite
corresponding keys on the target.

By default, `undefined` values in sources are excluded from the template.
entries.returns: A factory function that accepts source objects and returns an override function.

entries.name: mergeObjectsFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link MergeObjectsFunction} with a pre-configured filter.

Useful when you need to merge objects multiple times with the same filter configuration.
By default, `undefined` values are filtered out of the result (`KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`).

params.name: filter
params.description: - Optional filter controlling which key/value pairs are included. Defaults to filtering out `undefined` values.

entries.returns: A reusable merge function.

entries.name: findPOJOKeysFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link FindPOJOKeysFunction} with a pre-configured filter.

The returned function inspects each key/value pair on the input object and collects keys
whose values pass the filter.

params.name: filter
params.description: - A {@link FilterKeyValueTuplesInput } controlling which values match. Required (no default).

entries.returns: A reusable function that returns matching keys from any input object.

entries.name: countPOJOKeysFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link CountPOJOKeysFunction} with a pre-configured filter.

By default, counts all keys whose values are not `undefined` (`KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`).

params.name: filter
params.description: - A {@link FilterKeyValueTuplesInput } controlling which values are counted. Defaults to `KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`.

entries.returns: A reusable function that counts matching keys on any input object.

entries.name: filterFromPOJOFunction
entries.signature: filterFromPOJOFunction({ copy = false, filter: inputFilter = { valueFilter: KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED } }?: FilterFromPOJO<T>): FilterFromPOJOFunction<T>
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link FilterFromPOJOFunction} with a pre-configured filter and copy behavior.

The returned function removes key/value pairs that match the filter from the input object.
Internally, the filter is inverted so that matching values are deleted while non-matching values are retained.

By default, removes `undefined` values (`KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`) and does not copy (`copy: false`).

params.name: { copy = false, filter: inputFilter = { valueFilter: KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED } }

entries.returns: A reusable filter function. The returned function also accepts a `copyOverride` argument to override the copy behavior per-call.

entries.name: assignValuesToPOJOFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link AssignValuesToPOJOFunction} with a pre-configured filter and copy behavior.

The returned function assigns key/value pairs from a source object onto a target, skipping pairs
that are filtered out. By default, `undefined` values are filtered out (`KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`)
and the target is copied before assignment (`copy: true`).

params.name: input
params.description: - Filter and copy configuration. Defaults to `KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED` (filter undefined, copy target).

entries.returns: A reusable assign function with a `_returnCopyByDefault` property indicating the configured copy behavior.

entries.name: valuesFromPOJOFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link ValuesFromPOJOFunction} with a pre-configured filter.

The returned function iterates each key/value pair on the input object, collects values
from pairs that pass the filter, and returns them as an array.

By default, only non-`undefined` values are included (`KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`).

params.name: filter
params.description: - A {@link FilterKeyValueTuplesInput } controlling which values are included. Defaults to `KeyValueTypleValueFilter.UNDEFINED`.

entries.returns: A reusable function that extracts matching values from any input object.

entries.name: filterKeysOnPOJOFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FilterTuplesOnPOJOFunction} that retains only keys present in `keysToFilter`,
or excludes them if `invertFilter` is `true`.

Always returns a new object (never mutates the input).

params.name: keysToFilter
params.description: - The set of keys to include (or exclude when inverted).

params.name: invertFilter
params.description: - When `true`, keys in `keysToFilter` are excluded instead of included. Defaults to `false`.

entries.returns: Filters keys on any input POJO.

entries.name: filterTuplesOnPOJOFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FilterTuplesOnPOJOFunction} from a raw entry filter predicate.

The returned function iterates all entries of the input object, applies the predicate,
and returns a new object containing only the entries that pass.

params.name: filterTupleOnObject
params.description: - Predicate applied to each `[key, value]` entry.

entries.returns: Filters entries on any input POJO.

entries.name: forEachKeyValueOnPOJOFunction
entries.signature: forEachKeyValueOnPOJOFunction({ forEach, filter }: ForEachKeyValueOnPOJOConfig<T, C, K>): ForEachKeyValueOnPOJOFunction<T, C>
entries.description:
Creates a reusable function that iterates over filtered key/value pairs of a POJO,
invoking a callback for each matching pair.

This is the low-level building block used by {@link filterFromPOJOFunction}, {@link findPOJOKeysFunction},
{@link countPOJOKeysFunction}, {@link assignValuesToPOJOFunction}, and {@link valuesFromPOJOFunction}.

When no filter is provided, all key/value pairs are iterated.
entries.returns: Iterates matching key/value pairs on any input object.

entries.name: filterKeyValueTuplesFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable function that extracts and filters key/value tuples from objects.

When no filter is provided, returns all key/value tuples.

params.name: filter
params.description: - Optional filter configuration.

entries.returns: Extracts filtered tuples from any input object.

entries.name: filterKeyValueTupleFunction
entries.description:
Creates a filter predicate function for key/value tuples based on the provided filter configuration.

The predicate returns `true` for tuples whose values pass the configured filter. Supports value type filtering
(undefined, null, falsy, empty), key filtering, and inversion.

params.name: inputFilter
params.description: - Filter configuration (enum value or full config object)

entries.returns: A predicate function that tests individual key/value tuples.

entries.name: flattenObject
entries.signature: flattenObject(input: Maybe<Record<string, unknown>>, config?: FlattenObjectConfig): Record<string, unknown>
entries.description:
Flattens a nested object into a single-level object with concatenated keys.

Recursively traverses plain objects and joins nested keys with a separator (default `'.'`).
Non-plain-object values (arrays, Dates, null, functions, etc.) are treated as leaf values and kept as-is.
Empty nested objects are omitted from the result (they produce no keys).
Circular references are detected and treated as leaf values to avoid infinite recursion.

params.name: input
params.description: - The object to flatten. If nullish, returns an empty object.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for separator and max depth.

entries.returns: A new flat object with concatenated key paths.

entries.name: objectKeysEqualityComparatorFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link EqualityComparatorFunction} that compares two arrays of objects by extracting keys
and checking that both arrays contain the same set of keys (order-independent).

Returns `true` if both arrays have the same length and produce identical key sets.
Handles `null`/`undefined` inputs via {@link safeEqualityComparatorFunction}.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract one or more keys from each object.

entries.returns: An equality comparator for arrays of keyed objects.

entries.name: objectKeyEqualityComparatorFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link EqualityComparatorFunction} that compares two objects by extracting a single key
from each and checking strict equality.

Handles `null`/`undefined` inputs via {@link safeEqualityComparatorFunction}.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract the key from an object.

entries.returns: An equality comparator for keyed objects.

entries.name: mapObjectMapFunction
entries.signature: mapObjectMapFunction(mapFn: MapObjectMapValueFunction<M, I, O>): MapObjectMapFunction<M, I, O>
entries.description: Creates a reusable {@link MapObjectMapFunction} that applies {@link mapObjectMap} with the given mapping function.

params.name: mapFn
params.description: - Function that transforms each value (receives value and key)

entries.returns: Maps all values in an input object map.

entries.name: mapObjectKeysFunction
entries.description: Creates a reusable {@link MapObjectKeysFunction} that transforms the keys of an input object using the given mapping function.

params.name: mapKeyFn
params.description: - Function that computes the new key from the old key and its value.

entries.returns: Remaps keys on any input object.

entries.name: objectHasNoKeys
entries.description: Checks whether the object has no own enumerable keys.

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the object has zero keys.

entries.name: objectHasKey
entries.description: Checks whether the object has the specified own property using `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty`.

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check

params.name: key
params.description: - Property key to test for

entries.returns: `true` if the object has the key as an own property

entries.name: objectHasKeys
entries.signature: objectHasKeys(obj: T, keys: ObjectKey[], mode?: SetIncludesMode): boolean
entries.description: Checks whether the object has all or any of the specified keys, based on the mode.

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check

params.name: keys
params.description: - Keys to test for

params.name: mode
params.description: - Whether to require 'all' keys or just 'any'; defaults to 'all'

entries.returns: `true` if the keys match the mode criteria

entries.name: mapToObject
entries.description: Converts a Map to a plain object by iterating entries and assigning key-value pairs.

params.name: map
params.description: - Map to convert.

entries.returns: A plain object with the same key-value pairs.

entries.name: copyObject
entries.description: Creates a shallow copy of an object using the spread operator.

params.name: input
params.description: - Object to copy.

entries.returns: A new object with the same properties.

entries.name: SLASH_PATH_SEPARATOR
entries.description: The forward-slash character used to separate parts of a slash path.

entries.name: SLASH_PATH_FILE_TYPE_SEPARATOR
entries.description: The dot character used to separate the file extension from the file name within a slash path.

entries.name: DEFAULT_SLASH_PATH_ILLEGAL_CHARACTERS
