entries.description:
Pre-configured comparator that sorts strings in ascending order using `localeCompare`
with the `numeric` collation option enabled, so numeric substrings are compared by value
(e.g. `"2"` sorts before `"10"`).

params.name: a
params.description: - The first string to compare.

params.name: b
params.description: - The second string to compare.

entries.returns: A negative number when `a` sorts before `b`, positive when after, `0` when equal.
entries.example: ['10', '2', '1'].sort(compareStringsNumeric); // ['1', '2', '10']

entries.name: sortByStringFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link SortByStringFunction} that sorts values in ascending alphabetical order using `localeCompare`.

params.name: readStringFn
params.description: - Function to extract a string from each value for comparison.

entries.returns: A comparator function suitable for use with `Array.sort()`.

entries.name: joinStrings
entries.signature: joinStrings(input: Maybe<ArrayOrValue<Maybe<string>>>, joiner?: string, trim?: boolean): Maybe<string>
entries.description: Joins an array of strings into a single string. Trims and omits empty values.

params.name: input
params.description: string or array of strings

params.name: joiner
params.description: string to join the strings with. Defaults to a comma.

params.name: trim
params.description: whether or not to trim the strings before joining. Defaults to false.

entries.returns: joined string, or null/undefined if the input is null/undefined

entries.name: splitJoinRemainder
entries.signature: splitJoinRemainder(input: string, separator: string, limit: number): string[]
entries.description:
Splits a string like {@link String.prototype.split}, but joins overflow segments back together
instead of discarding them. Useful when you only want to split on the first N-1 occurrences.

params.name: input
params.description: - String to split.

params.name: separator
params.description: - Delimiter to split on.

params.name: limit
params.description: - Maximum number of resulting segments; overflow segments are rejoined with the separator.

entries.returns: Array of string segments, with length at most equal to limit.

entries.name: caseInsensitiveString
entries.description: Converts a string to its lowercase equivalent for case-insensitive comparison.

params.name: input
params.description: - string to convert to lowercase

entries.returns: the lowercased string, or undefined if the input is undefined

entries.name: capitalizeFirstLetter
entries.description: Capitalizes the first letter of the input string.

params.name: value
params.description: - String to capitalize.

entries.returns: The input string with its first character uppercased.

entries.name: lowercaseFirstLetter
entries.description: Lowercases the first letter of the input string.

params.name: value
params.description: - String to modify.

entries.returns: The input string with its first character lowercased.

entries.name: splitJoinNameString
entries.description:
Splits the input string into a first name and last name tuple using a space as the delimiter.
If the name contains more than one space, the remainder is treated as the last name.

params.name: input
params.description: - Full name string to split.

entries.returns: A tuple of [firstName, lastName], where lastName includes all text after the first space.

entries.name: nameToInitialsFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link NameToInitialsFunction} that derives display initials from a name or short character string.

Useful for avatar fallbacks where a name (e.g. `'Michelle B'`) or a literal token (e.g. `'BB'`)
should collapse to a compact label. By default a single-word input yields a single initial; raise
`minInitials` to pull more leading characters from a lone word.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration controlling the minimum and maximum number of initials.

entries.returns: A reusable function that derives initials from input names.

entries.name: nameToInitials
entries.description:
Derives display initials from a name or short character string using the default configuration.

Multi-word inputs use the first character of each of the first two words; single-word inputs use
the first character verbatim. The result is always uppercased.
entries.returns: Uppercased initials, or an empty string when the input is blank.
entries.example:
nameToInitials('Michelle B'); // 'MB'
nameToInitials('A'); // 'A'
nameToInitials('BB'); // 'B'
nameToInitials('Michelle'); // 'M'

entries.name: repeatString
entries.description: Creates a string that repeats the given string a specified number of times.

params.name: string
params.description: - The string to repeat.

params.name: reapeat
params.description: - Number of times to repeat the string.

entries.returns: The repeated string concatenation.

entries.name: cutStringFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link CutStringFunction} that truncates strings exceeding the configured maximum length.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration controlling max length and end text behavior.

entries.returns: A reusable function that truncates input strings.

entries.name: cutString
entries.signature: cutString(input: Maybe<string>, maxLength: number, endText?: Maybe<string>): Maybe<string>
entries.description: Truncates a string to the given maximum length, appending end text (defaults to "...") if truncated.

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to truncate, or null/undefined.

params.name: maxLength
params.description: - Maximum allowed length for the output string.

params.name: endText
params.description: - Text to append when truncated; defaults to "...".

entries.returns: The truncated string, or null/undefined if the input is null/undefined.

entries.name: flattenWhitespace
entries.description:
Collapses consecutive whitespace characters (excluding newlines) into a single space, then trims.

Newlines are preserved.

params.name: input
params.description: - String to flatten.

entries.returns: The string with collapsed whitespace.

entries.name: simplifyWhitespace
entries.description: Reduces multiple consecutive newlines to a single newline and collapses multiple whitespace characters to a single space.

params.name: input
params.description: - String to simplify.

entries.returns: The string with simplified whitespace and newlines.

entries.name: stringTrimFunction
entries.description: Trims leading and trailing whitespace from a string.

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to trim.

entries.returns: The trimmed string.

entries.name: stringToUppercaseFunction
entries.description: Converts a string to uppercase.

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to convert.

entries.returns: The uppercase string.

entries.name: stringToLowercaseFunction
entries.description: Converts a string to lowercase.

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to convert.

entries.returns: The lowercase string.

entries.name: transformStringFunction
entries.description:
Creates a string transformation function based on the provided configuration.
The resulting function will apply transformations in a specific order:
1. Trimming (if `config.trim` is true).
2. Slicing (if `config.slice` is provided).
2. Custom `config.transform` function (if provided).
3. Uppercase conversion (if `config.toUppercase` is true and no `config.transform`).
4. Lowercase conversion (if `config.toLowercase` is true and no `config.transform` or `config.toUppercase`).
If no transformations are specified, the identity function is returned.

params.name: config
params.description: - The `TransformStringFunctionConfig` detailing the transformations.

entries.returns: A `TransformStringFunction` that applies the configured transformations.

entries.name: addPrefixFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that adds a configured prefix to the input string if it does not exist on that string.

params.name: prefix
params.description: - The prefix to add.

entries.returns: Adds the prefix to a string.

entries.name: addSuffixFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that adds a configured suffix to the input string if it does not exist on that string.

params.name: suffix
params.description: - The suffix to add.

entries.returns: Adds the suffix to a string.

entries.name: padStartFunction
entries.description: Pads the start of a string to a minimum length.

params.name: minLength
params.description: - The minimum length of the string.

params.name: padCharacter
params.description: - The character to use for padding.

entries.returns: Pads the start of a string.

entries.name: sliceStringFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that slices and concats parts of a string based on the configuration.

params.name: config
params.description: - The configuration for the slice function.

entries.returns: A SliceStringFunction.

entries.name: splitStringTreeFactory
entries.description: Creates a {@link SplitStringTreeFactory} that builds tree structures by splitting strings on the configured separator.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration specifying the separator and optional metadata merge strategy.

entries.returns: A factory function that creates or extends split string trees.

entries.name: isolateWebsitePathFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IsolateWebsitePathFunction} that extracts and transforms a path from a website URL based on the configuration.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for path isolation, including base path removal, component range, query handling, and trailing slash behavior.

entries.returns: Isolates the configured portion of a website path.

entries.name: expandFlattenTreeFunction
entries.signature: expandFlattenTreeFunction(expand: ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>, flatten: FlattenTreeFunction<N, V>): ExpandFlattenTreeFunction<T, V>
entries.description:
Creates an ExpandFlattenTreeFunction by composing an expansion function and a flattening function.

This higher-order function takes a function to expand an array of values `T` into a list of trees (`N[]` where `N` is a TreeNode)
and another function to flatten these trees into a single array of values `V`.

params.name: expand
params.description: - An ExpandTreeFunction (values: T[]) => N[] that converts an array of T into an array of tree nodes N.

params.name: flatten
params.description: - A FlattenTreeFunction (tree: N, array?: V[]) => V[] that flattens a tree of N nodes into an array of V values.

entries.returns: An ExpandFlattenTreeFunction (values: T[]) => V[] that performs the combined expansion and flattening.

entries.name: expandTreeFunction
entries.signature: expandTreeFunction(config: ExpandTree<T> | ExpandTreeWithNodeBuilder<T, N>): ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>
entries.description:
Creates an ExpandTreeFunction using a basic ExpandTree configuration.
The resulting nodes will be of type TreeNode<T>.

Creates an ExpandTreeFunction using an ExpandTreeWithNodeBuilder configuration.
This allows for the creation of custom tree nodes of type N.

Implementation for expandTreeFunction. Creates a function that can expand a single value into a tree structure.

This factory function takes a configuration object that specifies how to retrieve children for any given value (`getChildren`)
and optionally, how to construct the nodes themselves (`makeNode`). If `makeNode` is not provided, a default node structure is used.
The returned function recursively builds a tree from a root value.

params.name: config
params.description: - An ExpandTree<T> or ExpandTreeWithNodeBuilder<T, N> configuration object.

entries.returns: An ExpandTreeFunction<T, N> that takes a root value and returns its corresponding tree structure.

entries.name: expandTrees
entries.signature: expandTrees(values: T[], expandFn: ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>): N[]
entries.description:
Convenience function for expanding multiple root values into an array of trees.
Each value in the input array is treated as a root for a new tree.

params.name: values
params.description: - The root values of type T to expand.

params.name: expandFn
params.description: - An ExpandTreeFunction<T, N> used to expand each value into a tree.

entries.returns: Array of N, where each N is the root node of an expanded tree.

entries.name: ExploreTreeVisitNodeDecision
entries.signature: const ExploreTreeVisitNodeDecision: { readonly VISIT_ALL: 0; readonly VISIT_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly VISIT_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }
entries.description: Decides how to visit a node during tree exploration.
entries.returns: { readonly VISIT_ALL: 0; readonly VISIT_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly VISIT_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }

entries.name: exploreTreeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable tree exploration function with configurable traversal, mapping, and filtering.

The returned function traverses one or more trees, applying a visit callback to each node.
By default uses depth-first traversal, identity mapping, and visits all nodes. All options
can be overridden per-call.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional default configuration for mapping, filtering, and traversal strategy.

entries.returns: A reusable function that explores trees with the configured behavior.

entries.name: depthFirstExploreTreeTraversalFactoryFunction
entries.description:
Creates a depth-first traversal strategy for tree exploration.

Visits each node before its children (pre-order). This is the default traversal
strategy used by {@link exploreTreeFunction}.
entries.returns: A traversal factory that processes nodes in depth-first order.

entries.name: breadthFirstExploreTreeTraversalFactoryFunction
entries.description:
Creates a breadth-first traversal strategy for tree exploration.

Visits nodes level by level, processing all nodes at depth N before moving to depth N+1.
Uses an internal queue to defer child processing until the current level is complete.
entries.returns: A traversal factory that processes nodes in breadth-first order.

entries.name: FlattenTreeAddNodeDecision
entries.signature: const FlattenTreeAddNodeDecision: { readonly ADD_ALL: 0; readonly ADD_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly ADD_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }
entries.description: Decides how to add a node to the flattened array during flattening.
entries.returns: { readonly ADD_ALL: 0; readonly ADD_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly ADD_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }

entries.name: flattenTree
entries.description: Flattens a tree into an array containing all its nodes using depth-first traversal.

params.name: tree
params.description: - The root node to flatten.

params.name: addNodeFn
params.description: - Optional filter controlling which nodes and subtrees are included.

entries.returns: The all nodes in the tree that pass the filter.
entries.example:
const nodes = flattenTree(rootNode);
// Returns [root, child1, leaf1, leaf2, child2, leaf3, child3]

entries.name: flattenTreeToArray
entries.signature: flattenTreeToArray(tree: N, array: N[], addNodeFn?: Maybe<FlattenTreeAddNodeDecisionFunction<N>>): N[]
entries.description:
Flattens a tree and appends the resulting nodes to an existing array.

Useful for accumulating nodes from multiple trees into a single collection.

params.name: tree
params.description: - The root node to flatten.

params.name: array
params.description: - The target array to push flattened nodes into.

params.name: addNodeFn
params.description: - Optional filter controlling which nodes and subtrees are included.

entries.returns: The same array reference, now containing the appended nodes.

entries.name: flattenTreeToArrayFunction
entries.signature: flattenTreeToArrayFunction(mapNodeFnOrConfig?: FlattenTreeToArrayFunctionConfig<N, V> | ((node: N) => V), defaultAddNodeFn?: Maybe<FlattenTreeAddNodeDecisionFunction<N, V>>): FlattenTreeFunction<N, V>
entries.description:
Creates a FlattenTreeFunction that flattens tree nodes themselves into an array.

Creates a FlattenTreeFunction that flattens tree nodes themselves into an array.

Creates a FlattenTreeFunction that flattens tree nodes into an array of mapped values.

Implementation for flattenTreeToArrayFunction.

Creates a reusable function that traverses trees and collects either the nodes themselves
or mapped values into an array. Supports both a simple function signature and a config object.

params.name: mapNodeFnOrConfig
params.description: - Optional mapping function or config object.

params.name: defaultAddNodeFn
params.description: - Optional default filter for node inclusion.

entries.returns: A reusable flattening function.

entries.name: latLngBoundTupleFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngBoundTupleFunction} that converts various bound inputs into a `[sw, ne]` tuple,
applying optional precision to the resulting points.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for point precision.

entries.returns: Produces bound tuples from flexible inputs.

entries.name: latLngBoundFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngBoundFunction} that normalizes various bound input formats into a {@link LatLngBound}.

Supports creating bounds from: two separate points, a `[sw, ne]` tuple, a four-point tuple (computes min/max extents),
or a pre-existing bound object.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for point precision and custom point functions.

entries.returns: Produces bounds from flexible inputs.

entries.name: isWithinLatLngBoundFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IsWithinLatLngBoundFunction} that checks if a given point or bound
falls entirely within the specified bound. Points are checked directly; bounds require
both corners to be within.

params.name: bound
params.description: - The reference bound to check containment against.

entries.returns: Returns `true` if the input is within the reference bound.

entries.name: overlapsLatLngBoundFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link OverlapsLatLngBoundFunction} that checks whether a given point or bound
overlaps the reference bound. Internally converts bounds to rectangles for overlap detection,
handling antimeridian wrapping.

params.name: bound
params.description: - The reference bound to check overlap against.

entries.returns: Returns `true` if the input overlaps the reference bound.

entries.name: build
entries.description:
Convenience function for imperatively constructing an object of a specific type by mutating a base object via a build function.

This is useful when building objects whose type is normally read-only, allowing incremental property assignment during construction.
entries.returns: The fully constructed object of type T.

entries.name: safeEqualityComparatorFunction
entries.description:
Wraps an {@link EqualityComparatorFunction} to safely handle `Maybe` values.

The wrapped function delegates to the comparator only when both values are non-nullish.
When both are nullish, it uses strict equality (`===`), so `null === null` is `true`
but `null === undefined` is `false`.

params.name: compare
params.description: - The comparator to wrap.

entries.returns: A new comparator that handles nullish values safely before delegating to the wrapped comparator.
entries.example:
const safeCompare = safeEqualityComparatorFunction((a: number, b: number) => a === b);
safeCompare(1, 1);       // true
safeCompare(null, null);  // true
safeCompare(null, undefined); // false

entries.name: safeCompareEquality
entries.signature: safeCompareEquality(a: Maybe<T>, b: Maybe<T>, compare: EqualityComparatorFunction<T>): boolean
entries.description:
Convenience function that safely compares two `Maybe` values using the provided comparator.

Delegates to {@link safeEqualityComparatorFunction} internally, so nullish values are handled
without invoking the comparator.

params.name: a
params.description: - First value to compare.

params.name: b
params.description: - Second value to compare.

params.name: compare
params.description: - The equality comparator for non-nullish values.

entries.returns: `true` if the values are considered equal.

entries.name: compareEqualityWithValueFromItemsFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link CompareEqualityWithValueFromItemsFunction} that extracts values from items before comparing them.

This is a convenience wrapper around {@link compareEqualityWithValueFromItemsFunctionFactory} that
accepts both the value reader and comparator in a single call.

params.name: readValues
params.description: - Extracts the comparable value from each item.

params.name: equalityComparator
params.description: - Compares the extracted values for equality.

entries.returns: Compares two items by their extracted values.

entries.name: compareEqualityWithValueFromItemsFunctionFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link CompareEqualityWithValueFromItemsFunctionFactory} that is pre-configured with a value reader.

The returned factory accepts different equality comparators, allowing reuse of the same value extraction logic
with varying comparison strategies.

params.name: readValues
params.description: - Extracts the comparable value from each item.

entries.returns: A factory function that accepts an equality comparator and produces a comparison function.

entries.name: decisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DecisionFunction} that always returns the given boolean, regardless of input.

Useful for providing a constant decision where a function is expected.

params.name: decision
params.description: - The constant boolean value to return.

entries.returns: A decision function that always returns the given boolean.
entries.example:
const alwaysTrue = decisionFunction(true);
alwaysTrue('anything'); // true

entries.name: asDecisionFunction
entries.signature: asDecisionFunction(valueOrFunction: Maybe<boolean | DecisionFunction<T>>, defaultIfUndefined?: boolean): DecisionFunction<T>
entries.description:
Normalizes a boolean value, a {@link DecisionFunction}, or undefined into a consistent {@link DecisionFunction}.

If the input is undefined, falls back to a constant function returning `defaultIfUndefined`.

params.name: valueOrFunction
params.description: - Boolean constant, {@link DecisionFunction }, or undefined to use the default.

params.name: defaultIfUndefined
params.description: - Fallback boolean when the input is nullish (defaults to true)

entries.returns: A {@link DecisionFunction } derived from the input.

entries.name: isEqualToValueDecisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DecisionFunction} that checks strict equality against the given value.

If the input is already a function, it is returned as-is, allowing callers to pass either a
concrete value or a custom decision function interchangeably.

params.name: equalityValue
params.description: - the value to compare against, or an existing decision function

entries.returns: a decision function that checks strict equality with the given value

entries.name: isEqualContext
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IsEqualContext} that captures a reference value and uses the provided comparator
to check whether subsequent values are equal to it.

params.name: contextValue
params.description: - The reference value to compare against.

params.name: fn
params.description: - The equality comparator.

entries.returns: Checks whether a given value equals the captured reference.

entries.name: areEqualContext
entries.description:
Creates an {@link AreEqualContext} that checks whether a single value or all values in an iterable
are equal to the captured reference value.

Returns `true` only if every value in the input matches the context value according to the comparator.

params.name: contextValue
params.description: - The reference value to compare against.

params.name: fn
params.description: - The equality comparator.

entries.returns: Checks whether all input values equal the captured reference.

entries.name: allObjectsAreEqual
entries.description:
Returns `true` if all values in the input are equal according to the provided comparator.

Empty iterables and single-value inputs return `true` by default, since there is nothing to contradict equality.
Uses the first value as the reference and checks all remaining values against it.

params.name: values
params.description: - The values to compare.

params.name: fn
params.description: - The equality comparator.

entries.returns: `true` if all values are equal to each other, or if fewer than two values are provided.

entries.name: sortAscendingIndexNumberRefFunction
entries.description: Creates a SortCompareFunction that sorts IndexRef values in ascending order by their index number.
entries.returns: A compare function suitable for Array.sort()

entries.name: indexDeltaGroupFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IndexDeltaGroupFunction} that separates items into new (no index), current (has index),
and deleted (present in previous but missing from current) groups. Useful for computing deltas when
items are added or removed from an indexed collection.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Reads an item's index, returning null/undefined for unindexed items.

entries.returns: Groups items by their index state.

entries.name: sortByIndexAscendingCompareFunction
entries.description: Creates a SortCompareFunction that sorts items in ascending order using a custom index reader.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Extracts the index number from each item.

entries.returns: A compare function suitable for Array.sort()

entries.name: computeNextFreeIndexFunction
entries.signature: computeNextFreeIndexFunction(readIndex: ReadIndexFunction<T>, nextIndex?: (value: T) => IndexNumber): ComputeNextFreeIndexFunction<T>
entries.description:
Creates a {@link ComputeNextFreeIndexFunction} that finds the maximum index in the input and returns the next available one.
Returns 0 when the input is empty.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Extracts the index number from each item.

params.name: nextIndex
params.description: - Optional custom function to compute the next index from the max item; defaults to max + 1.

entries.returns: Computes the next free index for a given array.

entries.name: computeNextFreeIndexOnSortedValuesFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link ComputeNextFreeIndexFunction} optimized for pre-sorted input arrays.
Instead of scanning all items for the maximum, it reads only the last element.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Extracts the index number from each item.

params.name: nextIndex
params.description: - Optional custom function to compute the next index from the last item; defaults to last + 1.

entries.returns: Computes the next free index from sorted arrays.

entries.name: minAndMaxIndexFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link MinAndMaxIndexFunction} that extracts the minimum and maximum index numbers from a collection.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Extracts the index number from each item.

entries.returns: The min/max indexes, or null for empty input.

entries.name: minAndMaxIndexItemsFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link MinAndMaxIndexItemsFunction} that returns the actual items (not just index numbers)
with the minimum and maximum index values.

params.name: readIndex
params.description: - Extracts the index number from each item.

entries.returns: The min/max items, or null for empty input.

entries.name: findBestIndexMatchFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FindBestIndexMatchFunction} from a set of IndexRef items.
Given an input index, returns the item with the highest index that is less than or equal to the input.

params.name: items
params.description: - The available match options; must not be empty.

entries.returns: Finds the best match for any input index.

entries.name: sortByIndexRangeAscendingCompareFunction
entries.description:
Creates a SortCompareFunction that sorts items by their IndexRange in ascending order.
Sorts by minIndex first, then by maxIndex for items with equal minIndex values.

params.name: readIndexRange
params.description: - Extracts the IndexRange from each item.

entries.returns: A compare function suitable for Array.sort()

entries.name: indexRangeReaderPairFactory
entries.description: Creates a new {@link IndexRangeReaderPairFactory} that pairs each value with its computed IndexRange.

params.name: reader
params.description: - Reads the IndexRange from the input value.

entries.returns: A factory that creates IndexRangeReaderPair instances.

entries.name: fitToIndexRangeFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link FitToIndexRangeFunction} that clamps index numbers to the given range boundaries.

params.name: input
params.description: - The range to clamp to.

entries.returns: Clamps any index to the range.

entries.name: wrapIndexRangeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link WrapIndexNumberFunction} that wraps index numbers around the range boundaries,
similar to modular arithmetic. Values that exceed the max wrap to the min side and vice versa.

params.name: input
params.description: - The index range to wrap within.

params.name: fencePosts
params.description: - Whether to use fencepost semantics (maxIndex is exclusive); defaults to true.

entries.returns: Wraps any index into the range.

entries.name: indexRangeCheckReaderFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IndexRefRangeCheckFunction} that reads an item's index and checks whether it falls
within the specified range. For IndexRef types, the index is read from `i` automatically.

Creates an {@link IndexRefRangeCheckFunction} that reads an item's index using a custom reader and checks whether it falls within the specified range.

params.name: input
params.description: - the range or range config to check against

params.name: read
params.description: - custom function to extract the index from the item

entries.returns: a function that checks whether an item's index is within the range

entries.name: indexRangeCheckFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IndexRangeCheckFunction} that tests whether an index number falls within the configured range.
The min is inclusive and the max is exclusive by default unless `inclusiveMaxIndex` is set.

params.name: input
params.description: - The range or range config to check against.

entries.returns: A predicate function for index numbers.

entries.name: isIndexNumberInIndexRangeFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IsIndexNumberInIndexRangeFunction} bound to the given range configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - The range or range config to bind.

entries.returns: A predicate that tests index numbers against the bound range.

entries.name: isIndexRangeInIndexRangeFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IsIndexRangeInIndexRangeFunction} bound to the given range configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - The bounding range or range config to bind.

entries.returns: A predicate that tests whether index ranges are fully contained.

entries.name: indexRangeOverlapsIndexRangeFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IndexRangeOverlapsIndexRangeFunction} bound to the given range configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - The reference range or range config to bind.

entries.returns: A predicate that tests for overlap with the bound range.

entries.name: stepsFromIndexFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link StepsFromIndexFunction} that computes the next index after stepping from a start position.
Returns undefined when the result falls outside the range (unless wrapping or fitting is enabled).

params.name: config
params.description: - Stepping behavior configuration.

entries.returns: Computes the stepped index.

entries.name: mapMaybeFunction
entries.description:
Wraps a MapFunction so that null/undefined inputs are passed through without invoking the map,
avoiding errors on nullable values.

params.name: mapFunction
params.description: - Function to apply only when the input is defined.

entries.returns: A new function that short-circuits on null/undefined inputs.

entries.name: mapArrayFunction
entries.description: Lifts a per-element MapFunction into one that operates on arrays, applying the mapping to each element.

params.name: mapFunction
params.description: - Per-element transformation.

entries.returns: Maps entire arrays.

entries.name: MAP_IDENTITY
entries.description:
The canonical identity MapFunction. Returns its input unchanged.

Used as a sentinel value so that {@link chainMapSameFunctions} and other combinators can detect
and skip no-op mappings for efficiency.
entries.returns: the same input value, unmodified

entries.name: mapIdentityFunction
entries.description: Returns the shared {@link MAP_IDENTITY} function cast to the requested type, useful for providing a typed no-op transformation.
entries.returns: The singleton identity function typed as `MapFunction<T, T>`

entries.name: isMapIdentityFunction
entries.signature: isMapIdentityFunction(fn: unknown): fn is typeof MAP_IDENTITY
entries.description: Checks whether the given function is the singleton {@link MAP_IDENTITY} reference.

params.name: fn
params.description: - The function to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the function is the identity singleton.

entries.name: chainMapSameFunctions
entries.description:
Chains together multiple MapSameFunctions into a single pipeline executed left-to-right.
Null/undefined entries and identity functions are automatically removed for efficiency.
Returns the identity function if no meaningful functions remain.

params.name: input
params.description: - One or more optional same-type map functions to chain.

entries.returns: A single composed function that runs all provided functions in order.

entries.name: chainMapFunction
entries.signature: chainMapFunction(a: MapFunction<I, O>, b: Maybe<MapFunction<O, B>>, apply?: boolean): MapFunction<I, O> | MapFunction<I, B>
entries.description:
Creates a single function that pipes the output of `a` into `b`.

If `apply` is false, or `b` is null/undefined, returns `a` unchanged. This conditional chaining
is useful when a second transform step is optional.

params.name: a
params.description: - the first map function

params.name: b
params.description: - the optional second map function to chain after `a`

params.name: apply
params.description: - when false, skips chaining and returns `a` directly

entries.returns: a composed function piping `a` into `b`, or `a` alone if `b` is absent or `apply` is false

entries.name: hasNonNullValue
entries.description: Type guard that returns `true` if the value is not `null` or `undefined`.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not `null` or `undefined`

entries.name: hasValueOrNotEmpty
entries.description:
Type guard that checks whether the input has a meaningful value.

Returns `false` for empty iterables (arrays, Sets, Maps), empty strings, and nullish values.
Non-iterable objects (e.g. `{}`) are considered non-empty by this function;
use {@link hasValueOrNotEmptyObject} to also reject empty objects.

NaN has undefined behavior.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is non-nullish and not empty.

entries.name: hasValueOrNotEmptyObject
entries.description:
Type guard that checks whether the input has a meaningful value, including checking for empty objects.

Returns `false` for empty iterables, empty strings, objects with no own keys (`{}`), and nullish values.
This is stricter than {@link hasValueOrNotEmpty}, which considers `{}` as having a value.

NaN has undefined behavior.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is non-nullish, non-empty, and not an empty object.

entries.name: isNotNullOrEmptyString
entries.signature: isNotNullOrEmptyString(value: Maybe<MaybeNot | '' | T>): value is MaybeSo<T>
entries.description:
Type guard that returns `true` if the input is not nullish and not an empty string.

Useful for filtering out both nullish values and empty strings in a single check.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not nullish and not an empty string.

entries.name: isMaybeNot
entries.description: Type guard that returns `true` if the input is `null` or `undefined`.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is `null` or `undefined`

entries.name: isMaybeSo
entries.description:
Type guard that returns `true` if the input is neither `null` nor `undefined`.

Equivalent to {@link hasNonNullValue} but with the `MaybeSo` narrowing type.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is neither `null` nor `undefined`

entries.name: isDefinedAndNotFalse
entries.description: Returns `true` if the input is not `null`, `undefined`, or `false`.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not `null`, `undefined`, or `false`

entries.name: isSameNonNullValue
entries.signature: isSameNonNullValue(a: Maybe<T>, b: Maybe<T>): a is NonNullable<T>
entries.description: Returns `true` if both inputs are non-nullish and strictly equal (`===`).

params.name: a
params.description: - First value.

params.name: b
params.description: - Second value.

entries.returns: `true` if both values are non-nullish and strictly equal.

entries.name: valuesAreBothNullishOrEquivalent
entries.description:
Returns `true` if both inputs are nullish (using loose equality `==`) or are strictly the same value.

This means `null` and `undefined` are considered equivalent to each other, but `false` and `null` are not.

params.name: a
params.description: - First value.

params.name: b
params.description: - Second value.

entries.returns: `true` if both are nullish or both are the same value.

entries.name: updateMaybeValue
entries.description:
Merges two `Maybe` values using `undefined` as the "no change" sentinel.

- If `b` is `undefined`, returns `a` (no update).
- If `b` is `null`, returns `null` (explicit clear).
- If `b` is defined, returns `b` (new value).

params.name: a
params.description: - The current value.

params.name: b
params.description: - The update value.

entries.returns: `a` if `b` is undefined, otherwise `b`

entries.name: modifier
entries.description: Creates a {@link Modifier} with the given key and modify function.

params.name: key
params.description: - Unique identifier for the modifier.

params.name: modify
params.description: - Function that mutates the target value.

entries.returns: A new {@link Modifier } pairing the key with the modify function.

entries.name: modifierMapToFunction
entries.description:
Converts a {@link ModifierMap} to a single {@link ModifierFunction} that applies all modifiers in sequence.

Returns {@link NOOP_MODIFIER} if the map is nullish or empty.

params.name: map
params.description: - The modifier map to convert.

entries.returns: A single modifier function that applies all mapped modifiers, or {@link NOOP_MODIFIER } if empty.

entries.name: maybeModifierMapToFunction
entries.description:
Converts a {@link ModifierMap} to a single {@link ModifierFunction} if the map is non-null.

Returns undefined if no map is provided, allowing callers to distinguish "no modifiers" from "empty modifiers".

params.name: map
params.description: - The modifier map to convert.

entries.returns: A composed modifier function, or `undefined` if no map is provided.

entries.name: latLngTupleFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngTupleFunction} that converts various input formats into `[lat, lng]` tuples,
applying optional precision configuration.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for precision and wrapping behavior.

entries.returns: Produces lat/lng tuples from flexible inputs.

entries.name: latLngPointPrecisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngPointPrecisionFunction} that rounds both lat and lng values
to the specified number of decimal places.

params.name: precision
params.description: - Number of decimal places to retain.

params.name: precisionRounding
params.description: - Optional rounding strategy (e.g., floor, ceil, round)

entries.returns: Rounds points to the given precision.

entries.name: latLngStringFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngStringFunction} that converts various input formats into comma-separated lat/lng strings,
applying optional precision configuration.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for precision and wrapping behavior.

entries.returns: Produces lat/lng strings from flexible inputs.

entries.name: latLngPointFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LatLngPointFunction} that normalizes various input formats (numbers, strings, tuples, objects)
into a {@link LatLngPoint}, with configurable precision, wrapping, and validation.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for precision, wrapping, validation, and tuple ordering.

entries.returns: Produces points from flexible inputs.

entries.name: validLatLngPointFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link ValidLatLngPointFunction} that returns the input point when valid, or a default point otherwise.

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - Factory for the fallback point; defaults to `defaultLatLngPoint`

entries.returns: A validation function.

entries.name: latLngDataPointFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link LatLngDataPointFunction} that wraps a {@link LatLngRef} object with its resolved point coordinates.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for precision and wrapping behavior.

entries.returns: Produces data points from lat/lng references.

entries.name: randomLatLngFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link RandomLatLngFactory} that generates random points within the specified bounding box.
The bounding box corners are capped/wrapped to valid coordinate ranges.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional bounding box and precision configuration.

entries.returns: A factory that produces random points within the bounds.

entries.name: randomLatLngFromCenterFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link RandomLatLngFactory} that generates random points within a rectangle
centered on the given point, extending by `latDistance` and `lngDistance` in each direction.

params.name: config
params.description: - Center point, distances, and optional precision.

entries.returns: A factory that produces random points near the center.

entries.name: mappedUseFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link MappedUseFunction} that transforms the input through the given `map` before applying the consumer.
If the mapped result is null/undefined, the default value is returned instead.

params.name: map
params.description: - Transforms the outer input into the type expected by the consumer.

entries.returns: A MappedUseFunction that maps then consumes.

entries.name: wrapUseFunction
entries.signature: wrapUseFunction(mappedUseFn: MappedUseFunction<A, B>, map: MapFunction<B, Maybe<I>>): MappedUseFunction<A, I>
entries.description:
Wraps an existing {@link MappedUseFunction} with an additional mapping step, allowing further transformation
of the intermediate value before it reaches the consumer.

params.name: mappedUseFn
params.description: - The existing mapped use function to wrap.

params.name: map
params.description: - Additional transformation applied to the intermediate value.

entries.returns: A new MappedUseFunction with the extra mapping layer.

entries.name: useContextFunction
entries.signature: useContextFunction(use: UseValue<I, O>, defaultValue?: GetterOrValue<O>): UseContextFunction<I>
entries.description:
Creates a {@link UseContextFunction} by binding a consumer and optional default value, so callers
only need to supply the input.

params.name: use
params.description: - The consumer function to bind.

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - Fallback when input is null/undefined.

entries.returns: A single-argument function that applies the bound consumer.

entries.name: mappedUseAsyncFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link MappedUseAsyncFunction} that transforms the input through the given `map` (which may return a Promise)
before applying the async consumer.

params.name: map
params.description: - Transforms the outer input, optionally asynchronously, into the type expected by the consumer.

entries.returns: A MappedUseAsyncFunction that maps then asynchronously consumes.

entries.name: wrapUseAsyncFunction
entries.signature: wrapUseAsyncFunction(mappedUsePromiseFn: MappedUseAsyncFunction<A, B>, map: MapFunction<B, Maybe<PromiseOrValue<Maybe<I>>>>): MappedUseAsyncFunction<A, I>
entries.description:
Wraps an existing {@link MappedUseAsyncFunction} with an additional async-capable mapping step,
allowing further transformation of the intermediate value before it reaches the consumer.

params.name: mappedUsePromiseFn
params.description: - The existing async mapped use function to wrap.

params.name: map
params.description: - Additional transformation (sync or async) applied to the intermediate value.

entries.returns: A new MappedUseAsyncFunction with the extra mapping layer.

entries.name: vectorMinimumSizeResizeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link VectorResizeFunction} that enforces minimum x/y dimensions.

For each axis, if a minimum is specified, the result uses whichever value is larger (input or minimum).
If a minimum is not specified for an axis, the input value is passed through unchanged.

params.name: minSize
params.description: - The minimum dimensions to enforce.

entries.returns: A resize function that clamps each axis to the specified minimum.
